chapter_title.en: Chapter on the Legal Punishment for Theft

text: 5110 - وَ رَوَى مُوسَى بْنُ بَكْرٍ عَنْ زُرَارَةَ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ اِكْتَرَى حِمَاراً وَ أَقْبَلَ إِلَى أَصْحَابِ اَلثِّيَابِ فَابْتَاعَ مِنْهُمْ ثَوْباً وَ تَرَكَ اَلْحِمَارَ عِنْدَهُمْ قَالَ "يُرَدُّ اَلْحِمَارُ عَلَى أَصْحَابِهِ وَ يُتْبَعُ اَلَّذِي ذَهَبَ بِالثَّوْبِ وَ لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ قَطْعٌ إِنَّمَا هِيَ خِيَانَةٌ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.5110 - Musa ibn Bakr narrated from Zurarah from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) who said:
I asked Imam (as) about a man who rented a donkey and went to cloth merchants, bought a garment from them, and left the donkey with them.
Imam (as) said: "The donkey should be returned to its owner, and the one who took the garment should be pursued. However, there is no cutting (of the hand) upon him because this is an act of betrayal, not theft."
